mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because rate cuts are then interpreted as accommodation rather than panic.
failure_condition: Reject if falling FEDFUNDS is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals demand deterioration and rising recession risk, which should compress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment jumps above the trigger but SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if the rank correlation with forward returns is not negative in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening consumer demand and labor income, which should translate into stronger revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly growth in RSAFS and PAYEMS does not precede above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the joint interaction adds no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stronger HOUST indicates a synchronized production and housing upturn, which should improve cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and HOUST do not show higher subsequent 60-day SPX returns than periods with flat or falling readings, or if the joint signal disappears after controlling for either proxy separately.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent, tighter policy is restrictive enough to dominate growth optimism, so higher policy rates should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month through discount-rate pressure and liquidity withdrawal.
failure_condition: Reject if the signal is not negative in high-rate regimes, specifically if 20-day SPX returns are positive in most observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 versus the prior quarter indicates broad demand softening and weaker future earnings growth, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 declines are followed by above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips after repeated growth slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend signals weakening payroll growth and reduced household income, which should depress near-term equity returns as revenue expectations and risk appetite deteriorate over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS changes are not associated with negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect only appears in conjunction with other series and not as a standalone labor signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS indicates inflation that forces policy restraint, so the combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month through higher real rates and lower valuation multiples.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% m/m and FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-plus-higher-rates episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.
